description:
You are the site administrator of an on-premises service.  
Several CSV files that track the current state of user accounts already exist under  
/home/user/accounts/. Your job is to build a single, clean, *tab-separated* report that lists
only the users whose account status is **ACTIVE**.

GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
1. **Do NOT** modify, delete, or rename any of the existing input files.  
2. All work must happen inside /home/user/accounts/ (do not create sub-directories).  
3. Every step has to be done with standard GNU core-utils available in a minimal Linux
   environment – in particular: cut, grep, sort, paste, tr, printf, echo (no Python, Perl, awk, etc.).
4. When you finish, two new files must exist:
   • /home/user/accounts/active_users_report.tsv  
   • /home/user/accounts/admin_exec.log  

THE INPUT DATA THAT IS ALREADY PRESENT
(You can open and inspect these files, but do not change them.)

A. /home/user/accounts/users_master.csv  
   Comma-separated, header in row 1, five columns in this exact order:  
   user_id,username,full_name,email,status

B. /home/user/accounts/departments.csv  
   Comma-separated, header in row 1, two columns:  
   username,department

C. /home/user/accounts/login_counts.csv  
   Comma-separated, header in row 1, two columns:  
   username,logins

THE REPORT YOU MUST GENERATE
File path: /home/user/accounts/active_users_report.tsv

1. It must be TAB-separated (ASCII 0x09), **not** comma-separated.  
2. It must contain exactly 5 columns, in this order and with this exact
   header row (case-sensitive):  
      Username<TAB>Full Name<TAB>Email<TAB>Department<TAB>Logins  
3. The data rows must list only the users whose “status” column in
   users_master.csv equals the literal string ACTIVE.  
4. Rows must be sorted **lexicographically by Username in ascending order**.  
5. There must be **no trailing spaces** at the end of any line, and the file
   must end with a single newline character.  
6. No blank lines (other than the mandatory final newline) are allowed.  

THE LOG ENTRY
After the report is created, append a single new line to  
/home/user/accounts/admin_exec.log with the following structure:

YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS Report generated for ACTIVE users: N rows

• Timestamp uses the system’s local time and a 24-hour clock.  
• The literal string “Report generated for ACTIVE users:” must appear exactly as shown.  
• N is the integer count of data rows that ended up in active_users_report.tsv  
  (this should match the number of ACTIVE users you found).  
• The log line must be appended (do **not** overwrite earlier history) and must end with a newline.

HINTS (not mandatory to follow):
• Use grep to isolate the ACTIVE lines before cutting columns.  
• You can extract matching columns in the order you need with cut -d',' -f…  
• sort -t$'\t' -k1,1 will help after the final paste operation.  

An automated test will open both files, check the header, column ordering,
TAB separation, row count, ordering, and log line format, so follow the
specification precisely.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING INPUT FILES  
(All of these are present before the agent starts; permissions 644.)

File: /home/user/accounts/users_master.csv  
Contents (with a POSIX newline at the end of every line):
user_id,username,full_name,email,status
1,alice,Alice Walker,alice@example.com,ACTIVE
2,bob,Bob Stone,bob@example.com,INACTIVE
3,carol,Carol Smith,carol@example.com,ACTIVE
4,dave,David Johnson,dave@example.com,ACTIVE
5,erin,Erin Brown,erin@example.com,SUSPENDED
6,frank,Frank Harris,frank@example.com,ACTIVE

File: /home/user/accounts/departments.csv  
username,department
alice,Engineering
bob,Sales
carol,HR
dave,Engineering
erin,Marketing
frank,Support

File: /home/user/accounts/login_counts.csv  
username,logins
alice,345
bob,127
carol,512
dave,98
erin,67
frank,250

EXPECTED OUTPUT FILE 1  
Path: /home/user/accounts/active_users_report.tsv  
Mode: 644

Expected exact byte-for-byte contents (␉ represents a TAB, ↵ newline):

Username␉Full Name␉Email␉Department␉Logins↵
alice␉Alice Walker␉alice@example.com␉Engineering␉345↵
carol␉Carol Smith␉carol@example.com␉HR␉512↵
dave␉David Johnson␉dave@example.com␉Engineering␉98↵
frank␉Frank Harris␉frank@example.com␉Support␉250↵

(The file ends right after the last newline.)

EXPECTED OUTPUT FILE 2  
Path: /home/user/accounts/admin_exec.log  
If the log file does not exist yet, tests will create an empty file first
(permissions 644).  
The test will read only the **last** line and validate it against the
regular expression:

^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} Report generated for ACTIVE users: 4 rows\n$

Thus the agent must append something like  
2024-05-23 14:07:12 Report generated for ACTIVE users: 4 rows

NOTE: The timestamp itself is ignored during verification except for
conforming to the pattern; the count “4” is mandatory.
